summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Canopi landing page - backend APIs working perfectly, frontend mostly functional with one mobile responsiveness issue

ui_bugs.issue: Mobile menu toggle not visible on mobile screens (display: none instead of display: flex)
ui_bugs.expected_behavior: Toggle should be visible on screens < 768px and hidden on screens >= 768px
ui_bugs.current_behavior: Toggle is hidden on all screen sizes due to CSS conflict
ui_bugs.impact: Users cannot access mobile navigation menu on small screens

passed_tests: Backend API root endpoint (/api/) returns correct response
POST /api/waitlist accepts email and returns success
GET /api/waitlist returns waitlist entries
POST /api/status creates status checks
GET /api/status returns status entries
Hero section renders with correct headline 'Predictive Intelligence for Reforestation'
Both hero CTAs present and clickable
Navbar navigation links scroll to correct sections (Problem, Solution, How It Works, About)
Problem section renders with 3 stat cards
Solution section shows 3 time horizon cards
How It Works section shows 3 connected steps
Who It's For section shows 2 audience cards
Why Now section shows 4 numbered points
Founder section shows Ana Gabriela Larios name and bio
LinkedIn link points to correct profile
Waitlist form accepts email and submits successfully to backend
Waitlist success message displays correctly after submission
Footer shows copyright 2026 Canopi and correct tagline
Footer social links (GitHub, LinkedIn, Email) present
Desktop navigation works correctly
Tablet responsiveness works correctly (nav links visible, mobile toggle hidden)
Scroll animations and section reveals function properly
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix mobile responsiveness: Mobile menu toggle should be visible on screens < 768px. Check CSS media queries in /app/frontend/src/App.css around line 188-191. The .navbar__mobile-toggle should have display: flex on mobile and display: none only on desktop/tablet.
success_percentage: Backend: 100% (5/5 tests passed), Frontend: 95% (22/23 tests passed)
